plan: In ForeignObject.deconstruct (django/db/models/fields/related.py, line 585), the string form of the relation is lowercased wholesale via self.remote_field.model.lower(), which also lowercases the app label (e.g. 'DJ_RegLogin.Category' becomes 'dj_reglogin.category'), so the lazy reference can never resolve for mixed-case app names. Fix: when the string contains a dot, split it into app_label and model_name and lowercase only the model_name ('%s.%s' % (app_label, model_name.lower())), otherwise lowercase the bare model name as before. This preserves the case-sensitive app label while keeping the lowercase model-name normalization that migrations expect.
